title: Stop pause process if runtime shuts down
body:
To avoid having a stale process running after invocations like `podman ps` we now shutdown the pause process on runtime shutdown.

@giuseppe do we have to add any checks for running pods/containers? I do not wanna break-up anything while removing the pause.pid

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *saschagrunert* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **jwhonce** You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @jwhonce` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Let's see if this breaks anything…
@saschagrunert looks like a few tests aren't hip

We call runtime.Shutdown() unconditionally at the end of all commands, so this is going to kill the pause process far too aggressively. It definitely needs to be gated on having running no running containers.
I agree and will add a check if no containers are running before shutdown.
Unfortunately I do not see a way forward with this PR, yet. Closing for now.
@giuseppe @haircommander Would the pinns work going on in CRI-O help with this at all?

> @giuseppe @haircommander Would the pinns work going on in CRI-O help with this at all?

no, rootless users are not able to create mounts required for pinning namespaces in the host mount ns.  It can be used only once we are already in the new user+mount namespace
Ok, I wonder if this is something we should bring up with the kernel team?  IE Is there a security problem without creating a mount to pin a namespace?

> Ok, I wonder if this is something we should bring up with the kernel team? IE Is there a security problem without creating a mount to pin a namespace?

it will make easier to leak namespaces.  Currently it is enough to kill all the processes for a user and we are sure there are no namespaces left around.

The problem still occurs on Ubuntu 20.10 and CentOS 7. In clustered computer environments programs should not left running in anode after a work completes. I am wondering what happens with SLURM if a job leaves a process running. Does it prevent job from completing or cause any other problems. Has this behavior been tested with SLURM?
We still don't have a way of reliably shutting down the pause process without racing against potential new instances of Podman starting, and this is unlikely to be fixed without that.
It seems the related feature request to systemd is still open, so I guess there is still hope that a feasible technical solution will be eventually found to fix this issue.
